title: Đề luyện tập 03 - RISC-V, Cache và Pipeline
description: Đề luyện tập 03. Mỗi câu 10 điểm, tổng 100 điểm.

questions.title: Execution time: single-cycle vs pipeline
questions.prompt:
Consider the following sequence of 32-bit RISC-V instructions:
add x9,  x1,  x2
lw  x10, 0(x9)
sub x11, x10, x3
lw  x12, 4(x9)
or  x13, x11, x12
sw  x13, 8(x9)
a) Single-cycle processor clock cycle time = 1000 ps.
b) 5-stage pipelined processor clock cycle time = 250 ps, without forwarding or hazard detection units. Insert NOPs to ensure correct execution.
c) 5-stage pipelined processor clock cycle time = 250 ps, with forwarding/hazard detection units.
d) Which case gives the medium performance?

fields.label: a) Execution time on single-cycle processor
b) Number of cycles after inserting NOPs
b) Execution time without forwarding/hazard detection
c) Number of stalls/NOPs needed with forwarding/hazard detection
c) Execution time with forwarding/hazard detection

questions.solution: Single-cycle: 6 × 1000 = 6000 ps. Without forwarding/hazard detection: one valid schedule uses 8 NOPs, so 14 instructions including NOPs => 14 + 4 = 18 cycles => 4500 ps. With forwarding/hazard detection, there are two load-use stalls: lw x10 -> sub and lw x12 -> or, so cycles = 6 + 4 + 2 = 12 => 3000 ps. The medium performance is case b.

questions.title: Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: sw
questions.prompt:
Consider a single-cycle RISC-V processor with the simple instruction set: add, sub, and, or, lw, sw, beq.

Let the processor execute the instruction:
sw x17, 192(x6)
at address 2048. Suppose the initial value of register xi is 48i for i = 1,...,31. Suppose the initial value of memory location with address M is M + 300.

Fill out the control signals and signals at locations A, B, C, ..., R.
questions.solution: For sw, RegWrite=0, ALUSrc=1, MemWrite=1, MemRead=0, ALUOp=00. x6=288 and x17=816. Effective address = 288 + 192 = 480. Branch target signal R follows PC + (imm << 1) = 2048 + 384 = 2432.

questions.title: Fully associative cache parameters
questions.prompt: Assume the main memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a fully associative cache with one-word blocks and a total size of 16 words. Assume one word is 32 bits.

fields.label: Number of blocks in the cache
Number of sets in the cache
Number of blocks in the memory
Number of tag bits in each block
Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Block size = 1 word, so cache blocks = 16/1 = 16. Fully associative => 1 set. Memory blocks = 2^8 / 1 = 256. Offset = 0, so tag = 8 bits. Total bits = 16 × (32 data + 8 tag + 1 valid) = 656 bits.

questions.title: 8-way set associative cache, byte addressing
questions.prompt:
Here is the cache with the following key characteristics:
8-Way Set Associative Cache
Write policy: Write-back using write allocate (one Dirty bit for a block)
Block size is 16 bytes
Cache size is 128 KB
Memory is byte addressing with 32-bit addresses.

Fill in the following values.

fields.label: Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Blocks = 128KB / 16B = 8192. Sets = 8192/8 = 1024, so set index = 10 bits. Offset = log2(16) = 4 bits. Tag = 32 - 10 - 4 = 18 bits. Total bits = 8192 × (16×8 + 18 + valid 1 + dirty 1) = 1212416 bits.

questions.title: 5-stage pipeline with forwarding and hazard detection
questions.prompt:
Assume that the following code sequence is executed on the 5-stage pipelined processor with forwarding and hazard detection units shown below:
add x14, x4,  x5
lw  x15, 8(x14)
lw  x16, 0(x14)
or  x15, x14, x15
sw  x15, 4(x14)
For the first seven cycles, specify the values of control signals at A, B, C, D, E, F, and G.
questions.solution: The dependency pattern is add -> lw base, then a later use of the loaded register by or, and then sw. With forwarding and hazard detection, the first seven control-signal rows follow the table shown after submission.

questions.title: 3-way set associative cache, điền Way theo từng dòng
questions.prompt:
Assume the memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a 3-way set associative cache with 2-word blocks and a total size of 48 words. Assume 1 word = 64 bits.

First, fill in the cache parameters. Then use true LRU. For each reference, identify binary address, tag, set index, offset, hit/miss, and the tags in each way after the reference has been handled.

Lưu ý: Nếu một Way có nhiều dòng như T(001)=0000, T(010)=1011, mỗi dòng sẽ là một ô nhập riêng.

Sequence of word addresses:
0x06, 0x84, 0x2d, 0x07, 0x8e, 0x5a, 0x8f, 0x1e, 0x3f, 0x85, 0x8f, 0xaa, 0x2f, 0xee

fields_intro.label: Number of blocks in the cache
Number of sets in the cache
Number of blocks in the memory
Number of tag bits in each block
Total bits required to implement the cache

fields_extra.label: After these accesses, is the cache full?

questions.solution: Cache blocks = 48/2 = 24. Sets = 24/3 = 8, so index = 3 bits. Offset = 1 bit. Tag = 8 - 3 - 1 = 4 bits. Hit/Miss sequence: M, M, M, H, M, M, H, M, M, H, H, M, M, M. Replacements = 2. Cache is not full because not every set has all three ways occupied. Total bits = 24 × (2×64 data + 4 tag + 1 valid) = 3192 bits. Các cột Way được giữ theo thứ tự block được đưa vào từng way vật lý; khi replacement xảy ra thì tag trong đúng way đó được thay, không sắp xếp lại theo set index.

questions.title: Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: lw
questions.prompt:
Consider the same single-cycle RISC-V processor.

Let the processor execute the instruction:
lw x14, 124(x8)
at address 2052. Suppose the initial value of register xi is 48i for i = 1,...,31. Suppose the initial value of memory location with address M is M + 300.
questions.solution: lw x14, 124(x8): x8 = 48×8 = 384, effective address = 384 + 124 = 508, memory data = 508 + 300 = 808. Instruction = 0x07C42703. For I-type lw, D is imm[4:0] = 11100, so the datapath still reads register x28 on the second read port; L = x28 = 48×28 = 1344. ALUSrc chooses the immediate 124 for the ALU input, so J=124 and K=508.

questions.title: Fully associative cache với true LRU
questions.prompt:
Assume the main memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a fully associative cache with two-word blocks and a total size of 8 words. Assume one word is 32 bits.

Sequence of word addresses:
0x06, 0x07, 0x20, 0x22, 0x06, 0x30, 0x23, 0x31, 0x40, 0x07, 0x21, 0x41, 0x50, 0x22

Assume a true LRU replacement policy. For each reference, identify the binary word address, the tag, and hit/miss.

fields_extra.label: After these accesses, is the cache full?

questions.solution: Block offset = 1 bit and tag = 7 bits. The cache has 8/2 = 4 blocks. Hit/Miss sequence: M, H, M, M, H, M, H, H, M, H, M, H, M, M. There are 4 replacements after the cache is full.

questions.title: Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: or
questions.prompt:
Consider the same single-cycle RISC-V processor.

Let the processor execute the instruction:
or x19, x7, x12
at address 2056. Suppose the initial value of register xi is 48i for i = 1,...,31. Suppose the initial value of memory location with address M is M + 300.
questions.solution: For R-type or, RegWrite=1, ALUSrc=0, MemtoReg=0, ALUOp=10, ALU control O=0001. x7=336 and x12=576, so ALU result = 336 OR 576 = 848.

questions.title: 2-way set associative cache, byte addressing
questions.prompt:
Here is the cache with the following key characteristics:
2-Way Set Associative Cache
Write policy: Write-back using write allocate (one Dirty bit for a block)
Block size is 64 bytes
Cache size is 32 KB
Memory is byte addressing with 32-bit addresses.

Fill in the following values.

fields.label: Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Blocks = 32KB / 64B = 512. Sets = 512/2 = 256, so index = 8 bits. Offset = 6 bits. Tag = 32 - 8 - 6 = 18 bits. Total bits = 512 × (64×8 + 18 + valid 1 + dirty 1) = 272384 bits.
